description_long: This is a dark green, twin-engine military airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a classic World War II-era design. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, each featuring a white cross insignia, and the engines are mounted on the wings with visible propellers. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with a multi-panel canopy, suggesting space for multiple crew members. The tail section features a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a conventional tail design. The landing gear is visible and consists of two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel, indicating a taildragger configuration. The airplane's surface is mostly matte with some insignia and markings, and the overall build suggests a metal construction typical of mid-20th-century military aircraft.
description: A dark green, twin-propeller military airplane with mid-mounted straight wings, a conventional tail, and visible landing gear. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned at the front, with white cross insignia on the wings and fuselage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin propellers, main landing gear, and the forward-positioned cockpit canopy.
description_view_1: Left-side oblique view highlighting the wing insignia, engine nacelles, and the tail section with its vertical stabilizer.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full wingspan, tail assembly, and the symmetry of the horizontal and vertical stabilizers.
description_view_3: Right-side oblique view showing the cockpit, both engines, and the taildragger landing gear configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main wheels under the wings and a single tail wheel, all fixed and non-retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-panel canopy, likely accommodating multiple crew members.
